 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.3.9","title":"Tests for handle_usage_limits (Codex): fixtures + synthetic workflow run with step logs","description":"# Task: Codex usage-limit workflow tests

## Goal
Prove correctness without relying on real account limits.

## Testing
- Fixtures for:
  - usage limit reached
  - session end summary
  - device auth prompt

- Synthetic workflow run:
  - validate step order
  - validate persistence updates
  - validate safety paths (pause/abort when policy denies)

- Drift handling:
  - fixtures for missing/partial fields
  - parser failures are structured and actionable

## Acceptance Criteria
- A synthetic run produces deterministic step logs and expected state transitions.
- Parsers succeed on fixtures and fail gracefully on drifted/missing fields.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.4","title":"[EPIC] Browser automation skeleton (Playwright profiles + OpenAI device auth)","description":"# Browser automation skeleton

## Goal
Provide the minimal browser automation required for Codex/OpenAI device auth.

## Design principles
- Prefer persistent browser profiles over stored passwords.
- Never log secrets.
- If Playwright hits MFA/password, fall back to interactive completion and then persist profile.



## Success Criteria
- Playwright-based browser automation supports persistent profiles and safe logging/artifacts.
- OpenAI device auth flow works for already-authenticated profiles and fails safely for MFA/password-required cases.
- A smoke-test command exists to validate browser automation on a real machine without embedding secrets.


## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - URL matching + state-machine transitions for the device-auth flow.
  - Redaction tests: ensure device codes / tokens never appear in logs or artifacts.

- Manual smoke test (required):
  - Provide a dedicated `wa browser smoke-test ...` command (see `wa-nu4.1.4.4`) that:
    - validates Playwright can launch with the configured profile dir
    - validates it can reach the device auth page
    - reports exactly what step failed with an artifact bundle

- Negative-path tests:
  - Explicitly exercise MFA/password-required cases and assert we fail safe with clear next steps.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.4.1","title":"BrowserAutomation scaffolding (Playwright init, profiles dir, safe logging)","description":"# Task: BrowserAutomation scaffolding

## Goal
Create the browser automation layer with correct lifecycle handling.

## Requirements
- Lazy Playwright initialization.
- Profiles directory under wa data dir:
  - `~/.local/share/wa/browser_profiles/<service>/<account>/`
- Headless vs non-headless modes:
  - default to non-headless during early development
  - headless possible once stable
- Safe logging:
  - never log device codes or secrets

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - profile path resolution is workspace/config correct
- Integration tests:
  - can launch a browser context and open a local page fixture

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can launch a browser context and open a page.
- Profile directories are deterministic and workspace-scoped.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.1.4.2","title":"Implement OpenAI/Codex device auth flow (auth.openai.com/codex/device)","description":"# Task: OpenAI device auth flow (Playwright)

## Goal
Automate the OpenAI device-code flow used by Codex CLI via Playwright in a way that is:
- robust to small UI changes
- safe with respect to secrets (no password storage)
- debuggable when it fails (screenshots/HTML dumps, redacted logs)

Target URL:
- `https://auth.openai.com/codex/device`

## Preconditions / inputs
- A persistent Playwright profile exists for the target account (profile dir derived from workspace/config).
- The workflow step has already obtained a `user_code` (device code) from the Codex pane.

## Happy path (v0)
1. Navigate to the device page.
2. Ensure we are in a logged-in state for the intended account:
   - if already logged in: proceed
   - if email prompt appears: fill email, continue
   - if password/MFA required: **do not attempt to automate**; exit into the fallback path (`wa-nu4.1.4.3`)
3. Fill the `user_code` form field.
4. Submit.
5. Verify success via a stable marker:
   - preferred: page contains “Successfully logged in” (or equivalent stable selector)
   - also capture URL + final DOM snapshot for debugging

## Failure modes
- Password/MFA prompts:
  - return a structured error that the workflow can treat as “interactive bootstrap required”
  - point to the fallback task (`wa-nu4.1.4.3`)
- Rate limit / bot detection / unexpected page:
  - fail with actionable error and attach redacted artifacts

## Logging / artifacts
- Never log passwords, session cookies, access tokens, or raw HTML that may contain secrets.
- On failure, write artifacts under the workspace artifacts dir:
  - screenshot
  - redacted DOM snapshot (or a hash + minimal selector debug)
  - a short, human-readable failure report

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - parse + validate the `user_code` format before browser automation (fail fast)
- Integration tests (offline):
  - run Playwright against a local HTML fixture that simulates the device page, verifying selector strategy
- Manual smoke tests:
  - a `wa browser smoke-test --openai-device` (tracked elsewhere) confirms it can complete on a real profile

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a valid persistent profile that is already authenticated, this flow completes and returns “success” for a real account.
- If password/MFA is required, the flow exits safely with a clear “interactive bootstrap required” error and produces redacted artifacts.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.1.4.3","title":"Profile persistence + MFA/password fallback (one-time interactive bootstrap)","description":"# Task: Profile persistence + MFA fallback

## Goal
Make auth reliable in the real world where MFA exists.

## Strategy
- Prefer persistent profiles so most auth runs require no credential entry.
- If Playwright detects password/MFA prompts:
  - open interactive browser window
  - prompt the operator to complete login once
  - then persist storage state for future automated runs

## Safety
- Never store passwords.
- Never log raw MFA codes, cookies, or OAuth redirect URLs with sensitive params.

## Testing
- Manual test plan:
  - first run requires interactive bootstrap
  - subsequent runs succeed without password prompts

## Acceptance Criteria
- After one interactive bootstrap, subsequent runs succeed without password prompts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.4.4","title":"Browser smoke-test command (manual) + failure reporting conventions","description":"# Task: Browser smoke-test command (manual)

## Goal
Provide a simple, human-invokable command to validate Playwright integration **outside** full workflows.

When selectors change (which they will), we want a fast feedback loop:
- run one command
- get a precise failure
- fix the automation

## Proposed CLI surface
- `wa auth test openai --account <name>`
  - exercises the OpenAI/Codex device auth flow end-to-end

- Optional flags:
  - `--profile <name>` (select persistent browser profile)
  - `--timeout-secs <n>`
  - `--headful` (default headless; headful for debugging)

## Output & error conventions
- Always return a structured outcome **matching the auth realities matrix** (`wa-nu4.1.4.5`):
  - `Success` → maps to `Automated`
  - `NeedsHuman` → maps to `NeedsHuman`
  - `Failed` → maps to `Fail`

- Failure output must include:
  - which step failed
  - current page URL (redacted)
  - recommended next action (update selector, run headful, etc.)

## Safety requirements
- Never ask for or store passwords.
- Redact URLs containing tokens/codes in logs.
- Always close contexts/pages on all code paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Running the command either succeeds or returns a precise error with next steps.
- Outcome categories are consistent with the auth realities matrix (no new ad-hoc states).
- Errors are stable and actionable (not vague “something failed”).


## Testing
- Manual test plan:
  - Run headless + headful modes.
  - Validate all three outcomes are reachable in controlled conditions:
    - Success (already-authenticated profile)
    - NeedsHuman (profile not authenticated / MFA)
    - Failed (simulate selector mismatch)

- Redaction tests:
  - Ensure logs/artifacts do not contain device codes or tokenized URLs.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.4.5","title":"Auth realities matrix — OpenAI/Codex","description":"# Task: Auth realities matrix — OpenAI/Codex

## Goal
Establish a **factual, repeatable** understanding of which OpenAI/Codex auth paths can be fully automated and which require a one-time human bootstrap.

This resolves PLAN.md Open Question #3 for the OpenAI/Codex path and prevents workflows from guessing.

## Why this matters (user value)
When a pane hits usage limits, wa must decide whether to:
- proceed automatically, or
- pause and request a human action

A documented matrix avoids brittle heuristics and makes failures explainable.

## Scope
- OpenAI/Codex device auth flow only (Phase 2).
- Manual exploration of **real-world states** and how to detect them:
  - already authenticated profile
  - needs device code entry
  - MFA/password required
  - SSO/enterprise redirect
  - rate-limited / captcha
- Outcome taxonomy to standardize across automation:
  - `Automated`
  - `NeedsHuman`
  - `Fail`

## Deliverables
1. **Auth realities matrix** (checked into docs or spec notes):
   - state name
   - detection signals (selectors / URLs / CLI prompts)
   - automation outcome (`Automated`, `NeedsHuman`, `Fail`)
   - required human steps (if any)
   - safe retry guidance

2. **Outcome taxonomy + workflow guidance**:
   - how `handle_usage_limits` should message `NeedsHuman`
   - how PolicyEngine should respond when auth is not automatable

3. **Redaction rules**:
   - define what must be scrubbed in auth artifacts (device codes, tokenized URLs)

> Note: Smoke-test alignment against this matrix is tracked in `wa-nu4.1.4.4`.

## Testing / validation (manual)
- Run manual auth checks in controlled scenarios:
  - already-authenticated profile → `Automated`
  - fresh profile → `NeedsHuman`
  - forced MFA/SSO (if available) → `NeedsHuman`

- Redaction checks:
  - notes/artifacts must not contain device codes or auth tokens

## Acceptance Criteria
- A documented matrix exists for **all OpenAI/Codex auth states** observed during testing.
- The outcome taxonomy is explicit and referenced by workflow fallback text.
- Redaction rules are written and unambiguous.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.5","title":"[EPIC] Accounts + usage integration (caut as source of truth)","description":"# Accounts + usage integration

## Goal
Integrate `caut` (coding_agent_usage_tracker) so wa can make quota-aware decisions.

## Core idea
`caut` is the usage truth; wa mirrors data for history and selection.

## Deliverables
- wrappers around `caut usage` and `caut refresh` (JSON)
- selection policy: highest percent remaining + LRU tie-break
- persistence to accounts table



## Success Criteria
- `caut` integration provides typed usage/account data and maps errors into actionable UX.
- Account selection is deterministic (threshold + LRU tie-break) and explainable.
- DB mirror supports listing/previewing selection decisions in robot/human surfaces.
- Unit/integration tests cover parsing, selection determinism, and DB updates.


## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - JSON parsing fixtures for `caut usage` and `caut refresh` outputs (including partial/malformed outputs and stderr noise).
  - Selection determinism tests (tie-breakers, thresholds, LRU updates).

- Integration tests:
  - Use a fake/fixture `caut` implementation to exercise the wrapper without hitting real accounts.
  - Verify DB mirror updates and robot/human views never expose secrets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:02:18.537651367Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T07:50:08.059932374Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T07:50:08.059857996Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-accounts","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.5","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.5","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.5","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":122,"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"All 5 children closed:
- .1 caut wrapper (CautClient with usage/refresh, typed errors, remediation)
- .2 Account selection policy (percent_remaining + LRU tie-break, explainable)
- .3 Accounts tests (31 unit tests covering parsing, selection, DB mirror)
- .4 Robot accounts list (wa robot accounts list --pick with pick preview)
- .5 Robot accounts refresh (wa robot accounts refresh, caut→DB upsert)

All deliverables met: caut integration, selection policy, DB mirror, robot commands.","created_at":"2026-01-29T07:49:57Z"}]}
@@ -309,11 +309,11 @@
 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.6.2","title":"Enforce reservations in PolicyEngine + send paths","description":"# Task: Enforce reservations in PolicyEngine + send paths

## Goal
Prevent unintended input injection into panes owned by someone else.

## Behavior
- PolicyEngine checks active reservation for the target pane.
- If reservation exists and owner != caller:
  - default: `RequireApproval` (human) or structured denial (robot/mcp)
  - include reservation metadata (owner, reason, expires_at) in the decision
- If caller is the owner, allow as normal.

## Integration points
- `wa robot send`
- `wa send`
- workflow step runner
- future MCP tools

## Audit
- Every reservation-based denial is recorded in audit trail (redacted).

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - owner can act
  - non-owner denied or requires approval
  - expired reservation treated as no reservation
- Integration tests:
  - policy decisions are stable and include reservation info

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reservations are consistently enforced across all action paths.
- Robot/MCP errors are stable and machine-parseable.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.6.3","title":"Robot/Human commands: reserve/release/list pane reservations","description":"# Task: Robot/Human commands for pane reservations

## Goal
Expose a clear API to acquire/release reservations and inspect who owns a pane.

## Robot mode (stable JSON)
- `wa robot reserve <pane_id> --ttl <secs> --reason <text>`
- `wa robot release <pane_id>`
- `wa robot reservations` (list active)

## Human CLI
- `wa reserve <pane_id> --ttl ...` (alias or subcommand)
- `wa reservations` (table view)

## UX requirements
- Errors must be explicit:
  - already reserved
  - reservation expired
  - invalid TTL
- Output includes:
  - owner
  - reason
  - expires_at

## Safety
- `reserve` / `release` must pass PolicyEngine (`ActionKind::ReservePane/ReleasePane`).
- Policy can require approval for reservations if configured.

## Integration
- `wa status` and `wa robot state` should surface reservation state for each pane.

## Testing
- Unit tests for command parsing and validation.
- Integration tests for reserve → list → release lifecycle.
- Policy tests: deny/require-approval for reserve/release when rules demand.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reservations can be created, inspected, and released via robot + human commands.
- Outputs are stable and documented in the robot schema.


LABELS: area-cli, area-robot, area-ux, phase-2

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.7.1: Robot mode scaffolding: CLI subcommands + stable JSON envelope + error codes ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.7.10: Robot JSON schemas: versioned envelope + per-command outputs, validated in tests ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.7.2: Implement `wa robot state` (live panes + DB enrichment + inferred agent/state) ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.6: [EPIC] Pane reservations (exclusive/TTL) for safe multi-step work ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.6.1: Reservation model + storage schema (pane_reservations) + expiry ● P2
  → ○ wa-4vx.8.1: Define policy model: ActionKind, PolicyDecision, authorize() API (capabilities provided separately) ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.8.4: Policy rules loaded from config (allow/deny/require approval) + robot-safe errors ● P1

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-nu4.1.6.4: Tests/E2E: pane reservations (TTL, conflicts, audit) ● P2
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T15:30:46.588477820Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T14:59:54.928653862Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T14:59:54.928508673Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-cli","area-robot","area-ux","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.3","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.3","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.3","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.7.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.3","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.3","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.3","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":126,"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented robot reservation commands in crates/wa/src/main.rs:

CLI commands:
- `wa robot reservations reserve <pane_id> --ttl <secs> --owner-kind <kind> --owner-id <id> [--reason <text>]`
- `wa robot reservations release <reservation_id>`
- `wa robot reservations list`

Data structs:
- RobotReservationInfo: id, pane_id, owner_kind, owner_id, reason, created_at, expires_at, released_at, status
- RobotReserveData: contains reservation info
- RobotReleaseData: reservation_id + released bool
- RobotReservationsListData: reservations list + total

Handler behavior:
- Reserve: calls storage.create_reservation() with TTL conversion (seconds→ms), returns conflict error with actionable hint
- Release: calls storage.release_reservation(), returns released boolean
- List: expires stale reservations first, then lists active ones
- All paths use robot JSON envelope with structured error codes
- Clean storage shutdown on all paths

Compiles clean, 22 robot tests pass.","created_at":"2026-01-29T08:14:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","title":"Tests/E2E: pane reservations (TTL, conflicts, audit)","description":"# Task: Tests/E2E — pane reservations

## Goal
Prove reservation behavior is correct, enforced, and visible with strong artifacts.

## Unit/integration tests
- Reserve → conflict → release lifecycle.
- TTL expiry converts active reservation to expired.
- Policy enforcement returns stable error codes and reasons.

## E2E scenario
- Reserve a pane via `wa robot reserve`.
- Attempt to send from a different owner (expect deny/require-approval).
- Release reservation and send again (expect allow).

## Artifacts
- Structured logs
- Audit trail extract (redacted)
- JSON outputs for robot commands

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Conflicts are deterministic and explainable.
- Audit records include reservation metadata (owner, reason, expires_at).

## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Ensure the E2E runner can intentionally fail the case when TTL expiry is not honored.
  - Assert artifacts include both the deny decision and the later allow decision.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T15:31:15.655152421Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T08:15:28.433046748Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T08:15:28.432976397Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-e2e","area-safety","area-tests","phase-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.8.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:04Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":127,"issue_id":"wa-nu4.1.6.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Reservation test coverage exists across two modules:

Storage tests (28 passing in storage::reservation_tests):
- Struct: is_active (valid/expired/released), serde round-trip
- Config: defaults, TTL clamping
- Create: basic, no-reason, conflict, post-release, different panes, TTL
- Release: sets released, idempotent
- Get active: returns Some/None correctly
- List: empty, multiple panes, excludes released
- Expire: stale/valid discrimination
- Round-trip: reserve→release→reserve, expired→new

Policy tests (4 passing):
- authorize_denies_reserved_pane
- authorize_allows_owning_workflow_on_reserved_pane
- risk_matrix_reserved_pane
- pane_capabilities_reservation_prevents_input

Robot commands compile and pass (22 robot tests).
E2E scenario deferred to wa-4vx.10 registry (requires running WezTerm).","created_at":"2026-01-29T08:15:13Z"}]}
-{"id":"wa-nu4.2","title":"[EPIC] Phase 3: Full agent support — complete packs + account rotation","description":"# Phase 3 — Full Agent Support

## Objective
Expand from “works for the main path” to “works broadly”:
- Complete patterns and workflows for **Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini**.
- Harden account rotation logic and integrate usage tracking as a first-class system.

## Why this phase exists
Agent CLIs change output formats over time; partial coverage creates fragility. This phase invests in:
- more complete detection packs
- richer structured extraction
- regression fixtures (“golden corpus”)
- workflow variants per agent

## Deliverables
- Rule packs:
  - `core.codex`: full coverage for usage warnings/reached/session summaries/auth prompts.
  - `core.claude_code`: compaction + banner + usage signals (as discovered in field).
  - `core.gemini`: usage reached + session summary + model changes + resume mechanics.

- Workflows:
  - `handle_usage_limits` implemented for all three agents (behavior may differ).
  - `handle_session_end` (capture + store structured session summaries).
  - `handle_auth_required` (centralize login flows).

- Accounts:
  - `caut` becomes the source of truth for usage; wa stores a historical mirror.
  - Selection policy (percent remaining + LRU tie-break).

## Acceptance criteria
- Primary detections for all three agents are covered by unit tests and golden corpus fixtures.
- At least one real-world end-to-end run exists for each agent’s usage-limit handling path (or documented safe fallback if truly impossible to automate).



## Success Criteria
- Rule packs and workflows expand to support all target agents (Codex, Claude Code, Gemini) with consistent behavior.
- Account rotation and agent-specific workflows are covered by regression tests and golden corpora.
- User-facing surfaces (robot/MCP/human CLI) remain stable as capabilities expand.


## Testing
- Golden corpus coverage:
  - Every new rule added in this phase must land with fixtures (positive + near-miss negative).
  - Corpus tests must fail loudly on drift and point to the exact rule + capture that changed.

- Cross-agent workflow regression tests:
  - Use shared workflow engine tests plus per-agent fixtures to validate semantics remain consistent (idempotency, resume, safe failure).

- E2E per-agent reality checks:
  - For each agent (Codex/Claude/Gemini), capture at least one real transcript and ensure it’s represented in fixtures.
  - If true automation is not possible (MFA), require an E2E “fails safe” scenario that still produces artifacts and instructions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.1","title":"[EPIC] Expand rule packs + golden corpus (Codex/Claude/Gemini)","description":"# Expand rule packs + golden corpus

## Goal
Move from "minimum viable patterns" to broad, resilient coverage.

## Why
Agents change output format over time. This epic institutionalizes:
- pack iteration discipline
- fixtures as source of truth
- low false-positive matching

## Deliverables
- Additional rules for all three agents.
- Corpus expansion and drift-handling workflow.



## Success Criteria
- Expanded packs cover real-world patterns for each agent with stable rule ids and minimal false positives.
- Golden corpus grows over time and prevents regressions across releases.
- Pack changes are validated by unit tests and corpus runner output is actionable.


## Testing
- Corpus discipline:
  - Every new rule requires fixtures that include:
    - positive matches
    - near-miss negatives
    - extraction assertions for structured facts

- Drift workflow:
  - When a real transcript drifts, the process is:
    1) add failing fixture
    2) adjust rule pack
    3) verify corpus runner + unit tests
    4) optionally add a second near-miss negative if the rule was loosened

- Tooling tests:
  - Pack linter/tests must validate rule IDs are stable, unique, and follow naming conventions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.1.1","title":"Expand core.codex rule pack + corpus (warnings, summaries, auth prompts, drift variants)","description":"# Task: Expand core.codex pack

## Goal
Increase coverage and robustness for Codex CLI outputs.

## Additions (examples)
- More usage warning formats / punctuation changes.
- Session end summary variants:
  - missing reasoning tokens
  - different ordering
  - commas/spaces changes
- Resume hint variants (`codex resume` vs alias forms).
- Device-auth variants (URL changes, prompt formatting).

## Deliverables
- New/updated rules in `core.codex`.
- Corpus fixtures for each variant.
- Negative fixtures to prevent false positives.

## Testing
- Golden corpus regression (see `wa-4vx.10.1`):
  - every new/changed rule must have at least one positive fixture
  - add negative fixtures for common false-positive traps
- Pack linter/drift workflow (see `wa-nu4.2.1.4`) enforces fixture coverage and stable IDs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Corpus suite passes and covers new variants.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.2.1.3","title":"Expand core.gemini rule pack + corpus (usage, session summaries, resume semantics, model changes)","description":"# Task: Expand core.gemini pack

## Goal
Capture Gemini CLI outputs robustly.

## Deliverables
- New rules for:
  - usage warnings/reached
  - session summary parsing
  - model changes
  - resume hints if present
- Fixtures to lock behavior.




## Acceptance Criteria
- New `core.gemini` rules are added for all listed deliverables (usage warnings/reached, session summary parsing, model changes, resume hints when present) with stable rule ids.
- Golden corpus fixtures are added/updated so:
  - the corpus runner fails loudly on drift
  - fixture diffs are actionable (rule id + expected vs actual)
- Known false positives in non-Gemini panes are covered by tests (agent gating/dedup semantics).


## Testing
- Fixture requirements:
  - For each new rule: positive fixture + near-miss negative fixture.
  - Include at least one “non-Gemini pane” fixture to validate agent gating.

- Drift workflow:
  - When Gemini output changes, the first step is adding a failing fixture; only then adjust patterns.

## Goal
Capture Gemini CLI outputs robustly.

## Deliverables
- New rules for:
  - usage warnings/reached
  - session summary parsing
  - model changes
  - resume hints if present
- Fixtures to lock behavior.




## Acceptance Criteria
- New `core.gemini` rules are added for all listed deliverables (usage warnings/reached, session summary parsing, model changes, resume hints when present) with stable rule ids.
- Golden corpus fixtures are added/updated so:
  - the corpus runner fails loudly on drift
  - fixture diffs are actionable (rule id + expected vs actual)
- Known false positives in non-Gemini panes are covered by tests (agent gating/dedup semantics).


## Testing
- Fixture requirements:
  - For each new rule: positive fixture + near-miss negative fixture.
  - Include at least one “non-Gemini pane” fixture to validate agent gating.

- Drift workflow:
  - When Gemini output changes, the first step is adding a failing fixture; only then adjust patterns.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.1.4","title":"Pack tooling: `wa robot rules list/test`, pack linter, drift workflow (fixture-first)","description":"# Task: Pack tooling + drift workflow

## Goal
Make rule evolution disciplined and fast.

## Deliverables
- `wa robot rules list` (emit rule ids + metadata)
- `wa robot rules test "<text>"` (emit match trace)
- Pack linter:
  - ensure stable ids
  - ensure each rule has at least one fixture or unit test
- Documented drift workflow:
  - capture failing output
  - add fixture
  - update rule
  - ship pack bump

## Testing
- Robot schema validation:
  - `wa robot rules list` output is validated against schemas
  - `wa robot rules test` output is validated against schemas

- Linter tests:
  - fixture-based tests for:
    - stable id enforcement
    - “each rule has fixture/unit test” enforcement
    - detection of invalid/slow regex patterns

- E2E coverage:
  - covered by `wa-4vx.10.24` (rules list/test + pack linter drift workflow)

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa robot rules list` returns a stable JSON output listing rule ids + metadata (no secrets) and is validated in tests.
- `wa robot rules test "<text>"` returns a stable JSON match trace suitable for debugging and is validated in tests.
- Pack linter exists and enforces:
  - stable ids
  - each rule has at least one fixture or unit test
  - no invalid/slow regex patterns without justification
- Drift workflow is documented and fixture-first, and contributors can follow it without consulting external docs.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.1.5","title":"Agent quirks + drift catalog (field-tested)","description":"# Task: Agent quirks + drift catalog (field-tested)

## Goal
Capture **undocumented behaviors** and output drift for Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini, then bake them into rule packs and regression fixtures.

This resolves PLAN.md Open Question #7 and prevents silent detection regressions.

## Scope
- Collect real-world snippets/banners/prompts that differ from current fixtures.
- Document quirks that affect detection or workflow gating:
  - unexpected banner variations
  - prompt markers missing or delayed
  - usage-limit text variants
  - alt-screen transitions

## Deliverables
1. **Quirks catalog** (short doc or section in pack notes):
   - agent
   - symptom / output pattern
   - detection impact
   - mitigation (rule update / guard change)

2. **Fixture updates**:
   - add sanitized transcripts to `tests/corpus/*`
   - include expected JSON detections

3. **Rule adjustments**:
   - update pack regexes with minimal, stable anchors
   - add negative fixtures for false positives

4. **Drift log**:
   - record date + source of drift
   - link to tests added

## Testing
- Golden corpus regression must include new fixtures.
- Add at least one negative fixture per new rule tweak.
- Ensure pack linter (`wa-nu4.2.1.4`) passes after updates.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Quirks catalog covers all three agents.
- Each documented quirk is tied to at least one new fixture.
- Rule updates are minimal and do not introduce new false positives.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.2.2","title":"[EPIC] Expand workflows for all agents (usage limits, session end, auth required)","description":"# Expand workflows for all agents

## Goal
Implement workflows beyond the initial Codex-first path:
- `handle_usage_limits` for Claude and Gemini (with safe fallbacks)
- `handle_session_end` to store session summaries
- `handle_auth_required` to centralize login triggers

## Deliverables
- robust per-agent state machines
- additional persistence fields in agent_sessions



## Success Criteria
- Agent-specific workflows (usage limits, session end, auth required) exist with consistent semantics and safe failure modes.
- Workflow execution remains policy-gated and auditable.
- Regression tests exist for each new workflow (fixtures + synthetic workflow runs) and E2E covers representative scenarios.


## Testing
- Regression test requirements per workflow:
  - Each workflow must have:
    - fixture-based detection inputs
    - a synthetic end-to-end workflow execution test (no real auth)
    - negative tests for guard failures (alt-screen, recent GAP, missing prompt)

- E2E requirements:
  - For each agent, run at least one representative scenario end-to-end (or a “fails safe with artifacts” scenario if automation isn’t possible).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Deliverables.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.
